customer_anxiety_profile: Financially strained homeowner who has been told before that solar won't save her much on a small house. She is motivated by bill pain and genuinely curious, but carries a prior evaluation that showed minimal savings — she has not been told why that evaluation might be wrong now. She is warm and cooperative but her commitment is soft: she said 'as long as it's not gonna take forever' and 'I would cancel it' in the same breath as confirming the time.
unresolved_concerns: Prior solar evaluation showed minimal savings for her 853 sq ft home — never probed or reframed; closer will face this cold
City of Denver financial assistance program for solar — Stacy mentioned she is actively considering this as an alternative; never acknowledged or positioned against Ion's program
Mother Gail Braemer is on the title — never confirmed whether Gail needs to be present for the appointment or is a required signatory; appointment was booked for Stacy alone

Bill collection deferred to email — Stacy was asked to send the first page of her Xcel Energy bill; bill not captured on the call; appointment confirmation was conditioned on receiving the bill ('once I get that usage from you, I'll send you a confirmation email')
sensitive_topics_raised: Active financial hardship — Stacy disclosed she is 'struggling' financially and that saving on electricity is driven by financial strain, not just preference
Credit score uncertainty — Stacy was unsure of her score and checked live on the call; found 679, just above the 670 threshold; this is a thin margin
exact_promises_rep_made: Specialist will drop by in person tomorrow (the 15th) at 10AM — approximately one hour blocked
Confirmation email with date and time will be sent once bill usage is received
Specialist will go over pricing, warranties, projected savings, and answer all questions
Specialist can customize the design to account for future EV charging needs
closer_should_know: Stacy was told years ago that solar wouldn't save her much on her small house — this is the live objection the closer must address first, with the actual design data in hand. She has not been told why the current evaluation might differ. If the closer walks in without addressing this, Stacy's prior experience becomes the frame for the entire conversation.
